title: Add intent-discovery dialogue protocol for vague prompts

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Make human intent discovery a first-class, bounded AW behavior for vague or under-specified prompts before Planning or implementation narrows the work.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not close #1197 with inference-only fields, generic clarification advice, or a partial slice. The final protocol must expose candidate interpretations, ask or visibly acknowledge uncertainty when stakes are high, prevent endless clarification loops, and feed clarified intent into existing AW surfaces.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.
canonical_core.next_action: Design the smallest protocol/skill/startup route that changes vague-prompt behavior without interrupting detailed or tiny direct work.
proof_expectations: Tests or generated references cover vague prompts, detailed issues, and direct small requests.
Validation shows the protocol output can carry clarified intent into task_intent, acceptance, durable_intent, Memory, Planning, or an issue.
touched_scope: tests and examples for ask, acknowledge-and-proceed, and direct-work cases
completion_criteria: AW provides a usable bounded dialogue protocol for vague prompts.
The protocol captures why the work matters, desired outcome, non-goals, acceptable first slice, uncertainty, and proceed-with-assumptions rule.
Startup or skill routing makes skipped clarification visible when inferred intent confidence is low and stakes_if_wrong is high.
Examples cover when to ask, when to acknowledge and proceed, and when not to interrupt direct work.

goal: Add a clear intent-discovery dialogue protocol for vague prompts before Planning or implementation silently collapses work into a convenient slice.
Expose candidate interpretations and uncertainty, then ask the user or proceed with explicit assumptions only when safe enough.
Carry clarified intent into existing task_intent, acceptance, durable_intent, Memory, Planning, or issue surfaces without storing excessive raw conversation history.
non_goals: Do not require clarification for every prompt.
Do not turn Planning into a chat facilitator or broad product-discovery system.
Do not store raw brainstorming history unless it materially helps continuation.
Do not close #1197 with only another inference field or generic clarification wording.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Agents should treat high-ambiguity intent as a workflow risk and use a bounded human dialogue path before shaping broad work.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Add intent-discovery dialogue protocol for vague prompts
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Counteract premature execution bias by making clarification the lower-cost path when stakes_if_wrong is high and inferred_intent_confidence is low.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Route #1197 into queued Planning with enough normalized intent and closure constraints for a later implementation pass.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Likely startup routing, skill/protocol docs, schema/default surfaces, and focused tests; choose concrete files during implementation.
execution_bounds.max changed files: Keep the first implementation slice small enough to prove the protocol is discoverable and usable.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: At minimum, run focused tests for startup/skill routing and the relevant workspace proof selected by implement --changed.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before broadening beyond the promoted item.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated backlog, adjacent modules, or canonical contracts not named by this plan.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, report AW smoothness/helpfulness gaps, better-way signals, unused-feature reflections, and places AW could help more. Route each concrete signal to exactly one owner class unless explicitly split, or mark no_signal_found after checking.
